what_it_does: Defines a global feedback action registry on window.MS_FEEDBACK_ACTIONS, provides registration/subscription/filtering helpers, and registers default context-menu actions for discussion, feedback capture, learning, iteration, pinning, and generated artifact refinement.
observations: The registry stores actions in a Map keyed by action id and notifies subscribers after register and unregister operations.
Registered actions receive defaults for group, appliesTo, order, and icon before being stored.
forTarget filters actions by target.type against appliesTo and sorts matching actions by numeric order.
Default actions use ui helpers to open chat, open capture dialogs, show toasts, and close menus.
Some actions write learnings through store.addLearning, including project-initiation pinning and generative prefer/avoid choices.
Generative actions share target labeling and scope fallback helpers.
standouts: Action behavior is fully declarative through registered objects with run callbacks.
The same registry supports interview feedback actions and generation feedback actions.
Learning actions encode user feedback into store.addLearning records with scope, kind, text, and context.
conventions: Uses a global window namespace with an immediately invoked function expression for encapsulation.
Uses plain objects for action declarations and callback-based UI/store integration.
Uses action groups, appliesTo arrays, and order numbers as menu organization metadata.
Uses fallback values for missing target labels and scopes.
concerns: register silently replaces an existing action with the same id.
Subscriber callbacks are invoked without error isolation.
run callbacks assume ui, store, and interview capabilities exist when the action uses them.
The returned _actions Map allows external mutation of registry internals.
notes: This file is the declarative registry and built-in action set for feedback and learning context-menu behavior.
questions: What target.type values are expected beyond the ones named in appliesTo arrays?
What guarantees exist for ui, store, and interview objects passed into action run callbacks?
Should duplicate action ids be intentional replacement behavior or guarded?
How are action groups rendered or separated by the context-menu UI?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file defines an extensible action registry and registers default feedback, learning, discussion, and iteration actions.
apparent_themes: Shared action registry for interview and generation flows
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make feedback commands extensible and reusable by letting menu entries be registered once, filtered by target type, and routed into UI capture, chat, iteration, or learning storage.
novelty: The registry treats feedback, teaching, redo, and generated-artifact preference controls as the same action shape, with learning records produced directly from context-menu choices.
contribution: This file contributes a neutral action-registry substrate for attaching contextual feedback, discussion, teaching, iteration, and generation-refinement commands to typed UI targets.
summary_for_embedding: Browser-side JavaScript feedback action registry that stores declarative context-menu actions, filters them by target type, and registers built-in discussion, feedback, learning, iteration, pinning, and generative refinement actions.
intention_for_embedding: It was for making feedback and teaching commands extensible across interview turns and generated outputs through a single action declaration and dispatch layer.

symbols.does: Registers or replaces one action object in the registry.
symbols.description: This function ignores falsy actions or actions without an id. For valid actions, it applies default metadata, stores the action by id, notifies subscribers, and returns the stored action object.

symbols.does: Registers each action in an array-like input.
symbols.description: This helper normalizes a missing input to an empty array and calls register for every entry. It is used by the default and generative action registration blocks to add several actions at once.

symbols.does: Removes an action by id and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: This function deletes the action id from the internal Map whether or not the id exists. It then calls every subscriber callback to signal that the registry contents may have changed.

symbols.does: Adds a registry-change subscriber and returns an unsubscribe function.
symbols.description: This function stores the callback in the internal Set of subscribers. The returned closure removes that same callback from the Set when called.

symbols.does: Returns actions applicable to a target, sorted by order.
symbols.description: This function reads target.type, keeps actions whose appliesTo includes '*' or that type, and sorts the result by ascending order. It is the registry's context-sensitive menu query path.

symbols.does: Builds a display label for a generative feedback target.
symbols.description: This helper prefers target.label, then ref.kindLabel, ref.name, or ref.kind. If none of those values exist, it returns the fallback label 'this'.

symbols.does: Finds the learning scope for a generative feedback target.
symbols.description: This helper returns target.ref.scope when present. If the target or scope is missing, it falls back to the scope string 'generation'.
